name: Low Fat Raspberry Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325 degrees F (165 C).
2. In a medium bowl, mix together the flour and brown sugar until well combined.
3. Add the margarine and walnuts; stir with a fork or pastry blender until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
4. Press the mixture into an ungreased 15x10x1-inch baking pan.
5. Bake for 10 to 15 minutes, or until lightly golden brown. Remove from oven and let cool.
6. In a large bowl, beat the cream cheese with vanilla until light and fluffy.
7. Add the marshmallow cream; mix just until combined.
8. Fold in the whipped topping until evenly incorporated. Refrigerate for about one hour, or until firm.
9. Sprinkle raspberries over the cheesecake filling.
10. In a medium saucepan, combine sugar, cornstarch, and water. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens and becomes clear.
11. Remove from heat; stir in the gelatin until dissolved.
12. Let the glaze cool for 20 to 30 minutes, or until lukewarm.
13. Carefully pour the glaze over the raspberries.
14. Refrigerate for about one hour, or until firm.
15. Cut into squares and serve. This recipe yields approximately 25 servings.
Nutrient data per serving: 259 calories; 3 g protein; 40 g carbohydrates; 10 g fat; 8 mg cholesterol; 136 mg sodium.
